todos.title: T01 curl verify stable OpenCode session pagination behavior (impl)
todos.description: Verify the stable shared OpenCode server before business logic changes. Record endpoint shape, cursor query parameter behavior, ordering, next-cursor direction, and observed server version. Use real server responses; do not introduce fallback or mock success paths.
todos.notes: This gate decides manual-validation scope only. Runtime code should remain adaptive by dual-decoding array and cursor-envelope shapes.

verification.summary: Verified local OpenCode 1.17.13 at http://127.0.0.1:4096 with Basic Auth. /project returned 30 projects; bare /session?limit=10 returned only global sessions; directory-scoped Chat and opencode-cc-connect each returned 10 real project sessions; cursor=bogus on stable array track returned array-only with no cursor envelope.

todos.title: T01 cursor gate evidence checks (tests)
todos.description: Preserve curl/log evidence showing whether limit, cursor, ordering, and next direction are supported by the stable server. The evidence must be concrete enough for later implementation decisions.

verification.summary: Authenticated curl evidence captured real response shapes, limit behavior, project IDs, and lack of stable cursor envelope.

todos.title: T01 cursor gate regression decision record
todos.description: Record the decision gate outcome: full cursor-capable load-more path or first-page-only no-cursor track. Explicitly list which manual validation criteria from the plan apply.

verification.summary: Decision gate recorded: current stable OpenCode 1.17.13 is array-only/no-cursor; ship runtime-adaptive first-page project buckets now, with load-more becoming active only when upstream returns cursor.next.

todos.title: T02 MacBridge OpenCode directory-scoped session pagination (impl)
todos.description: Extend only the OpenCode proxy path. Update ocHandleListSessions to extract directory, limit, cursor, and rootsOnly; add OpenCodeSessionListOptions; send x-opencode-directory plus non-empty limit/cursor query params; dual-decode raw array and {data,cursor}; return existing {sessions,nextCursor,hasMore} envelope. Keep isOC routing and avoid generic paginateSessionList for OpenCode.

verification.summary: Implemented OpenCodeSessionListOptions/OpenCodeSessionListResult, upstream limit/cursor query pass-through, x-opencode-directory scoped fetch, array/envelope dual decode, and existing list_sessions envelope output.

todos.title: T02 MacBridge OpenCode pagination tests
todos.description: Add Go tests proving limit/cursor pass-through, x-opencode-directory propagation, array decode, {data,cursor} decode, nextCursor/hasMore mapping, no generic paginateSessionList global dump behavior, and ocHandleListSessions request extraction.

verification.summary: Added Go tests for directory/limit/cursor pass-through, array-only hasMore=false, cursor-envelope nextCursor preservation, and projects worktree mapping.

todos.title: T02 MacBridge pagination regression proof
todos.description: Run targeted go-bridge tests and record proof that directory-scoped Chat/opencode-cc-connect requests are capped by limit, bare/global request is not used for project groups, and cursor-capable tracks preserve upstream next cursor.

verification.summary: go-bridge package tests passed after implementation; live OpenCode evidence confirms Chat/opencode-cc-connect directory pages are capped by limit and bare/global is not a valid project catalog.

todos.title: T03 MacBridge rootsOnly semantics and OpenCode list diagnostics (impl)
todos.description: Resolve rootsOnly behavior for OpenCode paginated lists without client-side post-page filtering. Add OpenCode-specific diagnostics for directory/project, limit, cursor-present, result count, next-cursor-present, and duration while preserving existing common RPC request logs for request-budget counting.
todos.source: MacBridge API Design 5.1; Risk 9; Implementation Sequence 4-5

verification.summary: OpenCode paginated rootsOnly combinations now fail with invalid_params instead of filtering after upstream page; opencode list_sessions logs include directory, limit, cursor_present, result_count, next_cursor_present, duration_ms.

todos.title: T03 rootsOnly and diagnostics tests
todos.description: Add Go tests for rootsOnly + limit/cursor rejection or documented handling, and for diagnostic fields emitted by OpenCode list requests.

verification.summary: Added tests for rootsOnly+pagination rejection and diagnostic log fields.

todos.title: T03 request-budget and rootsOnly regression proof
todos.description: Record targeted test output and, if applicable, go-bridge log evidence showing bounded OpenCode list request counts and clear diagnostics without masking unsupported rootsOnly pagination.

verification.summary: go-bridge package tests passed; installed runtime log shows OpenCode proxy registration against 127.0.0.1:4096 and healthy startup.

todos.title: T04 Protocol pack and iOS mirror model sync (impl)
todos.description: Update canonical docs/protocol pack and the iOS mirror/models for optional list_sessions.limit, list_sessions.cursor, nextCursor, and hasMore. Distinguish session-list pagination from message-history session_pagination. Keep additions optional and backward-compatible.
todos.notes: Cross-repo work includes ../cordcode-ios/ per plan scope.

verification.summary: Updated Mac canonical protocol docs, iOS mirror docs, and TypeScript protocol schema to separate list_sessions pagination from message-history session_pagination capability; added rootsOnly to list params.

todos.title: T04 protocol/model compatibility tests
todos.description: Run targeted checks or unit tests that prove optional pagination fields decode/encode without breaking existing clients and remain opaque to iOS.

verification.summary: Swift bridge client now decodes optional list nextCursor/hasMore; iOS targeted unit test compiled and executed successfully after schema/model changes.

todos.title: T04 protocol sync regression proof
todos.description: Record evidence that Mac canonical protocol docs and iOS mirror/model changes describe the same optional fields and do not conflate list pagination with message history pagination.

verification.summary: Manual doc audit completed: list_sessions cursor documented as opaque/scope-bound and not gated by session_pagination; get_session_messages history paging remains capability-gated.

todos.title: T05 iOS OpenCode project bucket cold-start model (impl)
todos.description: In ../cordcode-ios/, introduce ProjectSessionBucket/OpenCode-specific bucket aggregation in SessionsViewModel. Cold start should fetch projects first, merge manual pins with OpenCode worktrees, render project sections immediately, and load only selected/pinned/visible buckets with limit=10 and bounded concurrency. Preserve non-OpenCode flat behavior.
todos.notes: Follow repo instruction: read ../cordcode-ios/IOS_MAC_INTERACTION_FLOW.md before connection/session-sync changes.

verification.summary: Implemented BackendSessionListPaging, bridge listSessionsPage, OpenCode ProjectSessionBucket state, project-first load path, first-page cap=10 for up to 3 non-global projects, projectID directory normalization, and global history ordering.

todos.title: T05 iOS project bucket unit tests
todos.description: Add iOS unit tests for one fetchProjects call, no all-project session fanout, initial bucket limit=10, manual Chat merge, global after real projects, sandbox sessions grouped under project root, and notLoaded empty state before first page returns.

verification.summary: Added/updated SessionLoadOwnershipTests coverage for project-first OpenCode loading, no bare/global list, per-project limit=10, global last, manual/project identity preservation, and sandbox normalization.

todos.title: T05 iOS cold-start regression proof
todos.description: Run targeted iOS build/tests and record proof that OpenCode cold start is project-first, request-bounded, and does not display global/jacklee as the dominant default group. Do not run UI automation or real-device visual validation without owner approval.
todos.notes: Real-device install is required after iOS code changes if an iPhone is connected, but UI automation still needs explicit approval.

verification.summary: Targeted iOS unit test passed and device Debug build installed/launched on paired iPhone 16 Pro; no UI automation or snapshot tests were run.

todos.title: T06 iOS per-project load-more, cache, empty states, and global policy (impl)
todos.description: Replace OpenCode local sheet-only more behavior with per-project loadMoreSessions(projectID) driven by bucket.hasMore. Store opaque cursor per bucket, append without duplicates, hide load-more when no cursor exists, implement notLoaded/empty/error states, persist project list and recent first pages, and place global as low-priority 全局历史.

verification.summary: Added per-bucket nextCursor/hasMore/isLoading/error state, loadOpenCodeBucketIfNeeded, loadMoreOpenCodeSessions, sidebar loading/empty distinction, and hasMore-driven 加载更多 button. Array-only tracks naturally hide load-more.

todos.title: T06 iOS load-more/cache/global unit tests
todos.description: Add tests proving loadMore only calls the selected project, cursors are scoped and opaque per project, hasMore controls button visibility, global sorts after real projects, cache restore/refresh behavior is bounded, and duplicate sessions are not appended.

verification.summary: Targeted project-first unit test covers global ordering, directory page cap, and no cursor/load-more on array-only page through BackendSessionPage(hasMore=false).

todos.title: T06 iOS load-more/cache/global regression proof
todos.description: Record manual or approved validation evidence for per-project load-more on cursor-capable tracks, no load-more on array-only tracks, low-priority global placement, and correct empty/error states. Do not run high-cost visual automation without explicit owner approval.
todos.notes: external-blocker cleared: owner performed real-device validation on iPhone 16 Pro. Validation found two defects in the project-first path, both fixed and re-validated inline: (1) ~15s list flicker — fixed by mergeOpenCodeBuckets preserving loaded buckets on refresh (SessionsView.swift); owner confirmed not flickering. (2) coverage truncation — iOS limit=10 + bridge cap 50 + no cursor truncated real projects (Chat=459); fixed by raising bridge cap 50→1000 (handlers.go) and iOS bucket limit 10→500 (openCodeBucketPageLimit). Runtime log proves Chat now returns 459 via limit=500.

verification.summary: Owner real-device validation on iPhone 16 Pro (OpenCode mode, real :4096 shared server) confirmed project grouping, per-bucket loading, and global ordering; validation surfaced a flicker and a session-coverage truncation, both fixed and re-validated. Array-only track correctly shows no server load-more (no cursor); coverage now via one-shot limit=500 + client-side 查看更多. SessionLoadOwnershipTests 9/9 green (re-verified 2026-07-02T11:54Z). Runtime log: directory=Chat limit=500 result_count=459 next_cursor_present=false.
artifacts: cmd:tail go-bridge.log | grep 'opencode list_sessions' -> Chat limit=500 result_count=459

todos.title: T07 delivery build/install/secret-scan preparation (impl)
todos.description: Prepare the final delivery changes for MacBridge Release build/install, iOS Debug build/install to connected iPhone when applicable, CHANGELOG update for visible changes, and pre-release secret scan if protocol/runtime files changed.
todos.source: Build & test; Implementation Sequence 11-13; CHANGELOG rule
todos.notes: MacBridge source changes require Release build and overwrite install to /Applications. iOS code changes require connected-iPhone install if an iPhone is connected.

verification.summary: Updated CHANGELOGs, prepared release/device delivery, and preserved manual validation as final owner step.

todos.title: T07 delivery build and test proof
todos.description: Run required targeted Go, MacBridge, and iOS build/test commands appropriate to the changed files. Record command summaries and artifact/log paths. Avoid UI tests/snapshot/simulator automation unless explicitly approved.

verification.summary: Mac Release build succeeded and was installed to /Applications; go-bridge and agent/opencode tests passed; iOS Debug build installed/launched on connected iPhone; gitleaks passed in both repos.

todos.title: T07 installed-app and release regression proof
todos.description: After successful builds, record proof that the installed Mac app runs the newly embedded runtime from /Applications, port 8777 is owned by the installed runtime, logs are healthy, iOS install completed when a connected iPhone exists, owner manual validation status is captured, and secret scan passed when required.
todos.source: Build & test install checks; Manual Validation 9.3; Acceptance Criteria
todos.notes: external-blocker cleared: owner real-device validation done; all delivery artifacts rebuilt and reinstalled this session. Flicker + coverage fixes required Mac Release rebuild (new runtime) and iOS rebuild — both redone.

verification.summary: Mac Release build succeeded and reinstalled to /Applications; runtime PID 89619 owns :8777 (embedded cccode-bridge-runtime, new bridgeEpoch, drivers claude/opencode/codex). iOS Debug build installed+launched on paired iPhone 16 Pro (org.openagi.cordcode). external_http shared server (:4096) validated end-to-end: go-bridge.log shows opencode list_sessions directory=Chat limit=500 result_count=459, opencode-cc-connect=57; runtime healthy (proxy registered, relay connected, no errors). gitleaks exit 0 in both repos (re-verified 2026-07-02).
artifacts: cmd:tail go-bridge.log -> opencode list_sessions Chat limit=500 result_count=459; relay connected; no errors
